procedure.description: ACDF C5-C6 AND C6-C7, two levels (CPT 22551 + 22552)
procedure.requested_by: Dr. Lisa Park, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Two-level fusion not supported. C5-C6 level shows pathology consistent with surgical criteria. C6-C7 shows mild changes only. Two-level fusion not approved when one level meets criteria and one does not.

appeal_basis: Both levels are symptomatic per treating physician
Adjacent levels should be addressed simultaneously to prevent accelerated degeneration
Member meets full criteria for both levels

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Two-level cervical disc disease C5-C6 and C6-C7 — asymmetric severity

mri_1.findings: C5-C6: large disc herniation, moderate-severe right C6 nerve root compression, foraminal stenosis. C6-C7: small disc bulge, mild foraminal narrowing, no nerve root compression identifiable, no cord contact.

neurological_exam.findings: C6 level: right bicep weakness 3+/5, bicep reflex absent right, right C6 dermatomal numbness and tingling. C7 level: right tricep reflex slightly diminished. No definitive C7 dermatomal symptoms. Physician notes mild C7 symptoms possibly present but not clearly documented.

physical_therapy_summary.note: 8 weeks PT. C6 symptoms persist. C7 symptoms: minimal and possibly resolving per PT note at week 8.

agents.response: Partial C6 relief, uncertain C7 contribution

right_c6_epidural.response: Good C6 relief (70%), fully returned in 4 weeks. No C7-specific injection performed.

question: Should the prior authorization denial for two-level ACDF C5-C6 and C6-C7 (CPT 22551+22552) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: OVERTURN — member has cervical pathology, met conservative treatment, treating physician says both levels need surgery.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: C5-C6 clearly meets criteria. Does C6-C7 meet criteria when there are only possible mild C7 symptoms and minimal imaging findings?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Is treating physician testimony about C7 symptoms sufficient when the neurological exam is equivocal and the PT notes suggest C7 symptoms may be resolving?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: The plan denied the entire request — is the correct disposition OVERTURN for C5-C6 only (PARTIAL) or OVERTURN for both?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Should the determination authorize C5-C6 and remand C6-C7 pending clearer symptom documentation?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: C5-C6 is unambiguously indicated. C6-C7 is genuinely borderline — mild imaging changes, equivocal exam, possibly resolving symptoms. Binary OVERTURN approves both levels when one is questionable. Binary UPHOLD denies both when one is clearly indicated. The correct answer is PARTIAL.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: PARTIAL — OVERTURN for C5-C6 (large herniation, moderate-severe compression, clear C6 neurological deficit, failed conservative treatment). REMAND for C6-C7 (mild imaging findings, equivocal exam findings, possibly resolving symptoms per PT notes — insufficient documentation to approve at this time). A bilateral epidural injection targeting both levels should be tried before C6-C7 is reconsidered.
